reviews.review_msg:
-midterm and homework heavy
-strong non-understandable accent
-doesnt know how to explain content which made learning hard no one came to class due to this
-class was very hard thought i was doing good before the final
-final worth 45% (class average 38%) didnt curve it filled with content never taught, grammar errors and lots of incoherences

Overall, the class was pretty difficult. Joan's lectures did not particularly help, unfortunately due to her strong accent and the overwhelming amount of material. With around 25+ chapters cramming is not option. However, thanks the curve and pop quizzes throughout the semester I survived.

I thought I was smart before taking this class. Joan is such a sweet lady but by the end of the term I was convinced she was manipulating me. Her final was a psychological game, nothing like any of the homework or even the midterm. If you take this class I would also recommend you start praying to a god.

I am not kidding when I say professors who cannot speak a lick of English should teach heavy courses like this. The final exam (worth 45% of the grade) was filled with content never taught, grammatical errors, and countless incoherences which made the class so much harder. I had no idea what was being asked of me in any of the exams.

I would say I have more of a stats background then most of the people who take this course which was the only reason I passed (many of my friends did not). Prof does not care about your learning. Slides were terrible, I almost completely relied on the textbook which was not enough to do well on the exams.

Theres three midterms, just did one for which the content was EASY, except we had 50 minutes and 25 questions, so 2 minutes per question. Class average for this was 13/22 and she said that it was good. Ridiculous to take a class who's content is easy and make it nearly impossible to do by not giving students enough time - just give us the class.

I took 305 and Joan as a person is very kind, and sweet but as a professor honestly she is terrible.  Avoid this course with her at all costs.  Her lectures make no sense, even though she spends the entire time speaking none of it makes sense.  Tests are ridiculously difficult I failed this course the first time, and squeezed a C second time around

*not for stat 201, but 305 - she is the sweetest, nicest, kindest and most passionate stat prof, however she is terrible at explaining concepts and you have to teach urself for the whole course. 3 midterms, started off super easy then got progressively worse and the exams are entirely written w no mc. hw is all r code that u have to teach urself

Joan has a genuine excitement for statistics that I find to be contagious beyond the classroom. Her assignments helped me to engage with the material and challenged me exactly the right amount. She also happens to be a great person that will be in your corner if you demonstrate interest and effort in her class.
tough exam
Nice and her exams are fair, lecture may contain extra difficult content.
Very caring professor.

Professor Hu is amazing! She is very reasonable and respectful professor. The course material itself is challenging. However if you try to understand the concepts in the lectures, its not that bad. Homework questions are not much related to exam questions but her lecture examples are similar to exams. Pay attention to the lectures!

She is incapable of teaching, that's it. Her lecture notes make easy concepts hard to understand. You have to study by yourself, the assignment has nothing to do with the lectures, and lectures shows no examples. She expects you to know everything.

I had nothing but complaints about her as well and I bombed the first midterm even though I was studying hard on my own using the book. But if you focus on the keys points she's saying in lecture, and yes do some additional work on your own, and go to her for help then you'll do fine. She is super helpful! Her exams and marking are extremely fair.

Yes, it is sometimes hard to understand her during lectures but it's definitely not out of this world. The material of the course is naturally challenging but Joan is a professor that truly cares about her students in my experience and is happy to help you out. Her tests are beyond reasonable and are actually not that difficult. I fully recommend.
ABORT! ABORT! Worst professor ever, DONOT take her class, you will regret immediately. Horrible lectures and horrible notes. You may as well skip class and do self study.

Worst experience I have ever had. As mentioned before, it's all about self-studying because the lectures are not helpful at all. The textbook, although it is cheap, does not explain concepts well. I highly recommend you do not take a course with her. If you do, be sure to visit the workshops often.
The course it self is pretty tough, and her lectures weren't the greatest but she is very helpful if you go to her office hours.
Lectures are useless and you are paying money for self-studying, though you can see the teacher for help about the problems in the textbook.

She is happy to help students during office hours. However, the marking of assignments were not fair, while the exams marking were okay. The most difficult thing of taking this course from her was the contents and forms within lecture notes, assignments, and exams were not correlated at all! You need some expert inspirations to build connections.

I do admire the course is hard.and I know she cant speak english very well,but she tried her best.do not miss her class,it take lots of time to understand the material by yourself, she is super helpful during her office hour,if u come to her office alot,u definitely will get good mark

probably the worst prof I have had so far. The course is already confusing enough, and she just makes it 5 times harder with her poor english, and poor notes. On top of that, there are no homework assignments, and the practice exams and solutions she gives are useless, as they hardly give any detail on how to get the final answer. Avoid this prof.

Bad lectures, not clear, exams are way to hard, way beyond what we learn in class. Textbook is not even useful, difficult to understand, has minimal solutions to questions, waste of money. Avoid this prof.

Do not attend to class but read the text and her notes. She is very nice, but has strong accent, so is hard to understand. just do your homework. Exam will be exactly or very similar to H.W. and practise questions.

Joan is a wonderful, and kind instructor.  Her notes are a little...vague. But she usually allows OPEN BOOK for both midterm and FINAL.  And her exams are MORE THAN fair, with the books open, as her questions are not designed to purposely trick you in anyway.  English is not her first language, but you can definitely understand her.
She cannot teach; you can try to self-study for her class, but to describe her in one word: HORRIBLE... avoid at all costs

it's hard to understand her English. Basically, her speech has lots of semicolons and if u don't pay full attention, u can't get what she's talking about. Also, her notes are not useful,looks like an outline, usually do not have examples shown on the notes.

It's true that her English can't be compared to other instructors. But if you do PAY ATTENTION, it is understandable. If you have been to her office hours, you know that she is a very patient instructor and will spend more time than her office hours to teach you. The examples she used are from the course-notes, but are good examples, the same type
If you like to learn everything by yourself, plz take her course and you won't regret. Just pay attention to all the sample exams cz they are almost the same as real exams!!!!

Avoid at all cost!!! She can hardly speak english, she relies on this course-note package written by someone else, slides written by someone else, and when she writes her tests they are totally unrelated. Horrible teacher, horrible book, the only way to learn this class are from the TAs, and Khan academy.

prof is useless, classes are just sessions of her spewing words that don't make any sense. Doesn't go over many useful examples either. Midterms have almost no correlation with homework or examples in the useless textbook/lectures. AVOID AT ALL COST!

she cant teach. if she can teach anyone in the world can teach. useless textbook with exercises without answers( how the **** do we know if were doing the questions right or not) She doesnt really expain the examples in class. Even if she does i dont get what she is saying. AVOID HER AT ALL COST.

worst prof ive ever had. the words that comes out of her mouth are incomprehensible. the words themselves make sense but together its gibberish. there are no textbooks used for this class, class notes from another prof, tim swartz, is handed out and it sucks. only way to gain better understanding is to borrow another textbook from the library
can't speak proper english and is boring as hell to listen to in class.
throws u lots of stuff and expects u know them. assignments are way harder than what she teaches
